problem_text: Classify sum_{n=1}^infinity (-1)^(n+1)/(2n - 1) as absolutely convergent, conditionally convergent, or divergent.

content.label: Alternating series form
content.applies_when: the terms alternate in sign: a_n = (-1)^(n+1) b_n with b_n = 1/(2n - 1) > 0.

content.meaning: if b_n > 0, b_n is decreasing, and lim b_n = 0, then the alternating series sum (-1)^n b_n converges.

content.label: check b_n = 1/(2n - 1) is positive, decreasing, and lim b_n = 0, so the series converges (the Leibniz series for pi/4)
content.action: check b_n = 1/(2n - 1) is positive, decreasing, and lim b_n = 0, so the series converges (the Leibniz series for pi/4)
content.purpose: establish convergence of the alternating series

content.meaning: if lim a_n/b_n = c is finite and positive, sum a_n and sum b_n share convergence behavior; compare sum 1/(2n-1) with harmonic 1/n.

content.label: test sum 1/(2n - 1) by limit comparison with sum 1/n: lim [1/(2n-1)]/[1/n] = 1/2 > 0, and the harmonic series diverges, so sum 1/(2n-1) diverges
content.action: test sum 1/(2n - 1) by limit comparison with sum 1/n: lim [1/(2n-1)]/[1/n] = 1/2 > 0, and the harmonic series diverges, so sum 1/(2n-1) diverges
content.purpose: decide whether the convergence is absolute

content.label: Conditional convergence
content.applies_when: the series converges but the series of absolute values diverges.

content.label: the series converges but not absolutely, so it is conditionally convergent
content.action: the series converges but not absolutely, so it is conditionally convergent
